title: silene saxifraga subsp. fruticulosa
intro: Silene saxifraga subsp. fruticulosa, also known as the rock catchfly, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a popular remedy for wound care and skin issues such as acne and eczema. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing pain, inflammation, and fever, as well as promoting wound healing and improving respiratory health. Herbal preparations of Silene saxifraga subsp. fruticulosa can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat a range of health issues.
